text: The surgeon observes severe adhesions in the pelvis and proceeds to dissect the adhesions between the intestine and pelvic wall using monopolar electrocautery and scissors. The vagina is lifted by suturing to secure a better view of the pelvic area, ensuring careful manipulation to avoid damage to the small intestine.

The surgeon moves the intestine to the cranial side, exposing the hernia orifice. The hernia orifice measures seven centimeters by seven centimeters intraoperatively, and the surgeon aims to achieve a three-centimeter margin circumferentially around the orifice.

The surgeon shapes the ventral right ST mesh and inserts it into the abdomen through a port in the right lower quadrant. The mesh is then fixed laterally and posteriorly using absorbable tacks. The patency of the iliac arteries and veins is visually confirmed.
The surgeon sutures the mesh at the anterolateral side to prevent ureter damage, then expands the mesh to cover the pelvic floor, completing the laparoscopic hernia repair procedure.
